title: Hindutva mascot, ex-TMC insider: 5 reasons why BJP chose Suvendu Adhikari as Bengal CM
content: Suvendu Adhikari emerged as the BJP's choice for West Bengal Chief Minister after years of being the party's strongest anti-Mamata Banerjee face. His victories, including defeating Banerjee in her stronghold, solidified his position. Adhikari's insider knowledge of the TMC and ability to consolidate Hindu votes made him the natural leader for the BJP's first government in the state.

title: TTV asks governor to invite EPS to form govt; missing MLA found
content: Dhinakaran said there were media reports that his party had supported TVK. He alleged that there could be a forged letter purportedly supporting TVK.

title: Operation Sindoor: How close India and Pakistan came to the brink one year ago
content: Following a deadly terror attack in Kashmir, India launched precision strikes on militant camps in Pakistan, escalating tensions to a full military standoff. Despite Pakistan's nuclear threats, India maintained a strong stance, ultimately leading to a ceasefire after significant damage to Pakistani military assets.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn; gives 540 workers $240m
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: 'Suspension of all kinetic activity': Trump announces 3-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire
content: US President Trump announced a three-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire from May 9-11, hoping for a long-term peace deal and a prisoner exchange. Despite this, fighting persisted, with both sides reporting attacks. Ukraine accused Russia of continuing assaults, while Russia claimed to intercept numerous drones. The truce follows Russia's Victory Day, a proposal Kyiv had previously dismissed.

title: Can't hold fuel losses indefinitely: Officials; crude averages $105 a barrel in May so far
content: Soaring crude oil prices, averaging $105.4 a barrel, are straining oil companies' finances as they sell fuel below cost. Despite government excise duty cuts, losses are unsustainable. Experts suggest gradual price hikes and innovative strategies to manage the transition to renewables while supporting companies' investments and the macroeconomy.

title: 'You have 10 working days to voluntarily recognise unions': Google UK staff tells company
content: Google's DeepMind employees are demanding union recognition amid concerns over their AI technology's use by the US and Israeli militaries. Workers seek a commitment against developing AI weapons and surveillance tools, urging management to voluntarily recognize their unions or face legal action. The move highlights growing anxieties about AI's ethical implications and its potential for harm.

title: India conducts its maiden test of 'nuclear-capable ICBM' off Odisha coast
content: India's DRDO successfully test-fired a nuclear-capable intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) off the Odisha coast, a development that could place any country worldwide within its strike range. This achievement signifies India's mastery of complex rocket propulsion and guidance systems, enhancing its strategic deterrence capabilities.

title: Not 'actually tracking' anyone: Rs 7,000 GPS watches for Haj pilgrims stops working
content: Indian Haj pilgrims are questioning a mandatory Rs 7,000 GPS smartwatch, part of a Rs 10,000 airfare hike. Many devices, procured by the Haj Committee and supplied by Sekyo Innovations, reportedly fail to turn on or track pilgrims, causing distress, especially for the elderly. This initiative, aimed at safety, faces criticism over cost and execution.

title: Supreme Court in no mood to condone Madhya Pradesh minister in Col Sofiya Qureshi case
content: The Supreme Court expressed strong disapproval of Madhya Pradesh minister Kunwar Vijay Shah's controversial remarks, questioning the state government's decision on prosecuting him. Despite the Solicitor General suggesting the minister intended praise, the bench noted a lack of repentance and the statement's unfortunate impact. The court also directed the SIT to investigate other instances of the minister's objectionable statements.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to Trump Accounts, Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m to Texas University
content: Michael and Susan Dell are donating $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin to establish a new AI-focused medical center and research campus. This significant philanthropic gift, named UT Dell Medical Center, will integrate medicine, science, and computing, aiming to revolutionize healthcare through artificial intelligence.

title: We do not express opinion on correctness of arrest or non-arrest: SC
content: The Supreme Court emphasized its reluctance to direct arrests, prioritizing evidence collection and witness protection. While monitoring investigations, the bench declined to comment on the merits of the Anil Ambani Group (ADAG) case, citing past collateral damage to reputations. The CBI has filed nine FIRs totaling Rs 27,337 crore against ADAG entities.

title: Why Bombay HC upheld 2018 acquittal in 2005 fake encounter case of Sohrabuddin Shaikh: Explained
content: The Bombay High Court upheld a trial court's acquittal of 21 police officers and others in the Sohrabuddin Shaikh, Kausar Bi, and Tulsiram Prajapati alleged fake encounter case. The court found the prosecution failed to establish abduction, conspiracy, or murder, deeming the evidence insufficient to overturn the initial verdict. Appeals by Shaikh's brothers were dismissed.

title: 'Currently in therapy': Simone Biles opens up on mental health journey
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: Mike Vrabel seemingly upset after Dianna Russini mentions husband
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s alleged affair rumors refuse to die down even as the two remain distant from it and continue to deny their inappropriate relationship with one another, despite having families of their own, sparking outrage. Days after their picture from a New York City bar in 2020 sparked chaos, a new clip of Mike Vrabel being disappointed as Dianna Russini speaks about her husband has raised eyebrows.

title: Los Angeles Rams eye former Baltimore Ravens star DeAndre Hopkins to boost offense
content: ​The Los Angeles Rams have experienced a pretty successful run on the field in the last two seasons, with ranking 2nd in the NFL West in the 2025 season, sparking hope among fans. With 12 wins and 5 losses, the Los Angeles Rams faced a brutal loss against the Seattle Seahawks in the NFC Championship. Ahead of the new season, the Los Angeles Rams are focusing on fixing the roster.

title: India gets new labour law as Centre notifies wage code, caps work week at 48 hours
content: New labour codes have capped weekly working hours at 48, with overtime paid at double the hourly wage. Final rules for wages and industrial relations codes are out, paving the way for implementation. A National Reskilling Fund will support retrenched workers with new skills, financed by employers.

title: Voter support or MLAs' backing? Congress weighs its options in Kerala
content: Congress leadership is deliberating on the Kerala CM post, weighing intra-party support for KC Venugopal against public sentiment favoring VD Satheesan. A decision is expected by Sunday, with discussions involving key state leaders and central party chiefs in Delhi.

title: Catch-22 for visa applicants as US adds 2 more questions
content: US non-immigrant visa applicants now face new questions about experiencing or fearing harm in their home countries, with affirmative answers potentially leading to denial. This change could impact future asylum claims, as applicants might be challenged on the veracity of their statements. Immigration experts suggest these questions target legal immigrants who might later seek asylum.

title: Satheesan backers stage protests, Kerala Congress leadership steps in
content: Congress leadership intervened as supporters of opposition leader V D Satheesan held demonstrations across Kerala, demanding his appointment as the next chief minister. Some activists allegedly tore down posters of AICC leader K C Venugopal, also a contender for the post, prompting the KPCC to direct workers to refrain from such actions.

title: Hunt for killers of Suvendu's aide expands to UP, Odisha
content: Bengal CM-designate Suvendu Adhikari's aide Chandranath Rath's murder probe now includes contract killers from UP and Odisha. Sleuths are focusing on Montu Mondal, who was in the SUV during the attack and escaped. A car used in the crime was traced to a Matigara owner who'd advertised it for sale online.

title: In a 1st, NCRB tracks cases of cruelty against animals: 10,312 arrested in 2024
content: For the first time, the National Crime Records Bureau has tracked animal cruelty cases, registering 9,039 incidents and arresting over 10,000 individuals in 2024. Maharashtra led with the highest number of cases. The data reveals a high conviction rate for concluded cases but significant delays in judicial disposal.

title: Mark Hamill quote of the day: True heroes are defined by heart, not strength
content: Mark Hamill, renowned as Luke Skywalker, posits that genuine heroism is rooted in resilience and empathy rather than sheer force. His lasting impact in the Star Wars saga and philanthropic efforts highlights a deep sense of gratitude and a drive for social change. Hamill's insightful perspective remains a powerful beacon of inspiration across the globe.

title: Element of surprise key to secure strategic edge, Rajnath tells commanders
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh emphasized that future wars will hinge on innovative thinking and synergy, not just weaponry. He urged commanders to prioritize artificial intelligence, autonomous systems, and secure communications, stressing the need to anticipate enemy surprises and stay ahead.  Singh highlighted hybrid threats and multi-domain operations, commending the forces' jointness and technological adoption.

title: Patna-bound IndiGo flight makes emergency landing at Lucknow due to bad weather
content: An IndiGo flight from Kolkata to Patna made an emergency landing in Lucknow due to adverse weather conditions in Patna. The aircraft, carrying 180 passengers and six crew members, was diverted after being unable to receive landing clearance. The flight later departed for Patna once the weather improved.

title: Reform UK sees big gains as Labour gets hammered in local elections in England
content: Reform UK has emerged as a major force in English local elections, securing the most council seats and taking control of its first London council. This surge, alongside significant losses for Labour and Conservatives, signals a potential shift to a five-party political landscape. Labour faces historical setbacks, with its leader under pressure amid widespread voter dissatisfaction.

title: Finn Allen’s maiden IPL century powers KKR to dominant win over Delhi Capitals
content: Kolkata Knight Riders secured a commanding eight-wicket victory over Delhi Capitals, fueled by a stellar spin attack and Finn Allen's explosive maiden IPL century. This fourth consecutive win keeps KKR's playoff aspirations burning bright, while Delhi faces mounting pressure. Allen's unbeaten 100 off 47 balls guided KKR to a swift chase after restricting Delhi to 142.

title: Christian Gonzalez pushes for Stefon Diggs’ Patriots return
content: NFL stars Stefon Diggs and Tyreek Hill are currently without teams. However, both of them are sparking interest from their former teammates: Chris Jones urged Tyreek Hill to return to the Kansas City Chiefs, while Christian Gonzalez invited Stefon Diggs to the New England Patriots. This mirrors a past attempt by wide receiver Kendrick Bourne to recruit wideout Tee Higgins for the New England Patriots.

title: SIT files charge-sheet against man accused of bid on Farooq Abdullah’s life
content: A charge-sheet has been filed by the SIT against Kamal Singh Jamwal, 63, for the attempted assassination of former J&K CM Farooq Abdullah. Jamwal allegedly fired at Abdullah during a wedding ceremony in Jammu, driven by resentment over Kashmiri migrant issues. The SIT's investigation indicates a pre-meditated intent to target the politician.

title: 'Most hated man in Kolkata': Green reveals how he helped Allen complete IPL ton
content: Allen’s unbeaten 100 off 47 balls helped Kolkata Knight Riders register their fourth straight win in IPL 2026 on Friday. KKR chased down 143 in 14.2 overs to win by eight wickets.

title: Will rupee continue its free fall against US dollar or recover?
content: The Indian rupee is facing significant pressure, weakening against the US dollar due to soaring crude oil prices driven by Middle East geopolitical tensions and persistent foreign fund outflows. Despite India's robust economic growth, the currency's slide continues as the Reserve Bank of India intervenes to curb volatility rather than target a specific exchange rate.

title: Axar Patel enters unwanted record books, becomes second-worst captain to...
content: Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel's batting woes deepened in IPL 2026, marking a new low with the second-worst captain's average in the league's history. His struggles, coupled with a dismal strike-rate, highlight a significant concern for the team amidst a challenging season. KKR's spinners effectively stifled Delhi's batting lineup, leading to a batting collapse.

title: US adds stronger-than-expected 115,000 jobs in April despite Iran war impact
content: US employers added a robust 115,000 jobs in April, surpassing expectations despite global economic uncertainty from the Iran conflict. The unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. Healthcare and transportation sectors saw significant hiring, while manufacturing experienced job cuts. This resilient labor market suggests the Federal Reserve may hold off on interest rate cuts as inflation remains a concern.

title: 'Regardless of jersey colour': Kohli's pep talk after duck goes viral - Watch
content: Even after the defeat, RCB stayed in the top four. However, they still need better performances in their remaining four matches.

title: Happy Mother’s Day 2026: Top 50 wishes, quotes and messages to share
content: This Mother's Day, move beyond generic greetings to honor the women who raised you with meaningful messages. The article offers categorized heartfelt, witty, and minimalist quotes, acknowledging the "mental load" and celebrating "mothers of choice."

title: Decoding Bengal SIR data: Of 123 margins, 49 in closer focus
content: West Bengal's election saw a political earthquake, with BJP's massive win overshadowing concerns about Special Intensive Revision (SIR). Analysis reveals voter deletions exceeded winning margins in numerous seats, particularly in areas with significant supplementary deletions. While some seats showed clear BJP gains, others indicated TMC erosion without a direct BJP benefit, highlighting complex local dynamics.

title: India’s forex reserves fall by $7.79 billion to $690.69 billion
content: India's foreign exchange reserves experienced a significant drop of $7.794 billion, reaching $690.693 billion in the week ending May 1. This decline, primarily driven by a fall in foreign currency assets and gold reserves, follows a previous decrease. The central bank's intervention in the forex market to support the rupee is a key factor behind this trend.

title: Real Madrid fine Tchouameni and Valverde $588,000 each after training-ground fight
content: The club said no sporting punishment would be given to the two players and added that the fine “thereby concludes the internal procedures” started after the incident.

title: Mirwaiz expresses concern over closure of school-cum-seminary under UAPA in Kashmir
content: “The banning of the Jamia Siraj Ul Uloom school under the stringent Unlawful Activities Prevention Act (UAPA) is a serious matter. It has stoked apprehension among people that our religious identity is being targeted,” said the Mirwaiz

title: Panel lawyers appointed for bonded labour cases in Thanjavur
content: Seven lawyers in Thanjavur were appointed partly due to the volume of cases and partly because they had undergone specialised training imparted by the International Justice Mission

title: Punjab worse than West Bengal under AAP rule, says Haryana CM Saini
content: Taking on the AAP dispensation in Punjab, Nayab Singh Saini alleged that they are busy in corruption and people are fed up while industrialists are shifting

title: Rat poison in watermelon killed four of a family in Mumbai: forensic report
content: Further probe is being conducted by the JJ Marg police station, which has recorded statements of kin and neighbours

title: Nitish and BJP have no moral right to speak on dynastic politics, says Tejashwi Yadav
content: Tejashwi Yadav pointed out that without contesting any elections or holding membership in any house, Nitish Kumar’s son, Nishant, and Upendra Kushwaha’s son, Deepak Prakash, have been appointed Ministers

title: Jabalpur cruise boat tragedy: Nightmare on the Narmada
content: Of the 41 passengers and crew members onboard a cruise vessel on the Narmada River, 13 died when it capsized during a violent storm in the Bargi Dam reservoir on April 30. The tourism department-operated cruise had been carrying families seeking respite from the summer heat. Mehul Malpani reports on the tragedy in Jabalpur and the questions it has raised over safety protocols and preparedness for inland vessel operations

title: Tamil Nadu government formation LIVE: AMMK chief Dhinakaran refutes party's support to TVK
content: Dhinakaran accused the TVK of submitting a "forged" letter to the Governor and slammed them for horse trading; TVK has reportedly denied these charges and accused Mr. Dhinakaran of spreading "false news"

title: Only Congress party can defeat BJP in the battle of ideologies, says Rahul Gandhi
content: Rahul Gandhi joins Haryana Congress leader’s ‘Sadbhav Yatra’, accuses BJP of attacking democratic system and Ambedkar’s Constitution

title: Congress slams Modi Government over women’s safety citing NCRB data
content: Mallikarjun Kharge said NCRB data pointed that cybercrime had surged by 1,689% and 10,546 farmers, 52,931 daily wage labourers and 14,488 students died by suicide in 2024

title: CBI files charge sheet against Shubhkamna Buildtech and others
content: The CBI is currently investigating 50 cases registered pursuant to the directions of the Supreme Court against various builder companies and financial institutions across the country in matters concerning cheating and diversion of funds involving homebuyers

title: BJP’s victory in Bengal will plug a big hole in national security, says Amit Shah
content: The victory in West Bengal is significant because after 100 years of ideological continuity, we have a government from every state from Gangotri to Gangasagar, says Shah at meeting of legislators in Kolkata

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: Left backs TVK but VCK undecided as uncertainty continues
content: Vijay meets Governor for the third time with tally of 116, two short of majority; AMMK’s Dhinakaran alleges horse-trading; Left leaders cite need to form a popularly elected government by May 10 to prevent the imposition of President’s Rule

title: 3,352 candidates forfeit deposit in T.N. Assembly polls
content: Among the major political parties, the AIADMK candidates lost their deposit in 19 constituencies, while the BJP candidates lost their deposits in five

title: SC/ST representation beyond reserved seats still remains limited, show T.N. Assembly election results
content: Barring the AIADMK front, the other 3 fronts fielded SC/ST candidates in general constituencies. In such contests, the lone MLA elect was P. Viswanathan of the Congress, who won from Melur

title: Tamil Nadu Congress MLAs camping in Hyderabad amid uncertainty in government formation
content: The five MLAs reportedly arrived on Thursday (May 7, 2026) and planned to leave after reports came in that Tamil Nadu Governor Rajendra Arlekar had asked Mr. Vijay to form the government

title: SC calls for renovation, restoration of Devaraj Urs Market, Lansdowne building
content: The Supreme Court’s observations follow an evaluation report submitted by the IIT Roorkee on the assessment of the condition of the two heritage buildings of Mysuru, which the State Government had proposed to demolish and rebuild

title: Tree-felling proposal sparks massive backlash; Forest Dept. gets 2,300 objections
content: Thursday was the last day for the public to submit objections and suggestions regarding the proposed tree-felling, which includes a large number of roadside and median trees along key stretches in the city

title: Calcutta High Court to hear pleas against Great Nicobar project in June; overrules Centre’s objections
content: HC dismisses Centre’s claim that petitioner has no locus standi, noting that she is espousing the cause of vulnerable tribals; says project of “national importance” and high cost must still follow the law

title: Final decision on Kerala CM turns into political cliff-hanger as ball now in Congress High Command’s court
content: The question of who among the three probables – V.D. Satheesan, K.C. Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala – will make the cut appears set to go down to the wire, with High Command reportedly scheduled to make pivotal announcement on May 10

title: Suvendu Adhikari to become the first BJP Chief Minister of West Bengal
content: Chief Minister-elect Suvendu Adhikari reached Kolkata Lok Bhavan and met Governor R.N. Ravi to stake claim to form the government. He will take oath as the Chief Minister of West Bengal at Kolkata’s Brigade Parade Grounds on Saturday

title: West Bengal government formation updates: Suvendu Adhikari meets Bengal Governor, stakes claim to form government
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah, who presided over BJP legislature party meeting said that all the proposals were received in favour of Suvendu Adhikari.

title: Watch: Vijay meets Governor Arlekar for third time after CPI, CPI(M) support | Above the Fold | 08.05.2026
content: From Tamil Nadu’s dramatic government formation talks and China’s fresh remarks on Operation Sindoor to West Bengal getting its first BJP Chief Minister - Top stories of the day.

title: Engine failure delays Nagapattinam-Kankesanthurai ferry by over four hours
content: According to sources, the ferry developed engine failure near Kallar, about two nautical miles off the Nagapattinam coast, and remained stranded at sea for some time amid rough weather conditions

title: Bangladesh failed to give ‘actionable response’ to diplomatic notes: India
content: In diplomatic note on April 30, New Delhi says it sent over 1,000 messages to Dhaka on repatriation of illegal immigrants since 2020, but the neighbouring country has not extended necessary cooperation

title: Khed court acquits Anil Parab, four others in Dapoli resort case
content: The judgment, delivered on May 5 by judge Prasanna S Chandgude, dismissed charges of cheating, forgery and misconduct

title: SC takes cognisance of resolution about HC judge sending advocate to custody
content: The SCBA had urged Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, in a resolution passed on May 6, to look into the incident while expressing shock at the judge’s reported conduct

title: Plea in Supreme Court says Governor is ‘duty-bound’ to invite TVK to form government in Tamil Nadu
content: The petition, filed by advocate A. Lakshminarayanan, said such a move by the Governor would “sound the death-knell for democracy” and be against the mandate of the electorate

title: Census enumerators ID can be verified through QR code
content: Lalit Jain, Director of Census Operations (DCO), Haryana seeks to allay concerns among residents in high-rise apartment complexes that are refusing to allow entry to enumerators

title: TCS case: Nida Khan sent to police custody till May 11; AIMIM corporator booked for harbouring her
content: The police have also booked an AIMIM corporator from Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar on the charges of allegedly harbouring Ms. Khan while she absconded from the law

title: Educational institutions must ensure bus safety compliance by May 20, warn officials
content: Inspections of buses operated by educational institutions began on May 1 and will cover 1,571 buses across the NTR district

title: SC criticises Punjab govt. for inability to curb drug menace
content: Drug abuse and trafficking continue galore as only small peddlers were caught while none dared to lay a hand on the big fish who ran the racket, says CJI-led Bench

title: Karnataka among top three States in corruption: NCRB
content: The State has the most number of Disproportionate Assets cases at 75, followed by Odisha with 62 and Assam with 44 cases

title: CPI and CPI(M) tell Tamil Nadu Governor their support is for stable secular government
content: The letter was sent under the instruction of the party's State secretary, M. Veerapandian and the legislature party leader T. Ramachandran

title: ‘Extremely shy’ to direct arrest as first option, no need to sensationalise probe: SC in Anil Ambani’s ADAG ‘bank fraud’ case
content: CBI report estimates loss to public sector banks and LIC at over ₹27,000 crore in seven cases involving Anil Ambani’s ADAG and its firms. Top court says arrest must be last resort, and not first option.

title: Prosecution failed to establish ‘conspiracy’ to kill Sohrabuddin Sheikh: Bombay HC
content: The court said that not a single witness identified the accused in the dock as the persons who had abducted Sohrabuddin Sheikh, Kausar Bi and Tulsiram Prajapati

title: Can't sit next to Congress in Lok Sabha anymore, DMK tells Speaker
content: the DMK decision has raised the question of if the party is also leaving the INDIA bloc . the seating plan in LS was jointly negotiated by the bloc after the 2024 LS elections . many of the MPs sat alongside members of the main opposition party, Congress .

title: Axar Patel begins 'next year' talk as DC playoff hopes fade after KKR loss
content: At TOI Sports Desk, reporters work around the clock to bring you comprehensive updates from the world of sports. Expect nuanced match reports, previews, and reviews, along with statistics-based technical analysis, the latest social media trends, and expert insights across cricket, football, tennis, badminton, hockey, motorsports, wrestling, boxing, shooting, athletics, and more. title: Ka$h and Carry: Patel's FBI goes full happy hour
content: the bureau has been distributing personalized bottles of woodford Reserve engraved with "Kash Patel FBI Director" he denies the allegations of excessive drinking . the bourbon allegedly traveled aboard DOJ aircraft, appeared at official events, and even traveled to Milan during the winter Olympics .

title: Glow hard: Trump's nuclear threat grows darker in face of Iranian defiance
content: both sides exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz on friday amid a diplomatic impasse . the global community appeared to be coming to a realization that Washington may have overestimated its hand in the conflict . signs of Iranian resilience and defiance prompted Trump to issue what was seen as another veiled nuclear threat .

title: TVK Claims MLA Kamaraj Wrote Letter In support, Refutes AMMK Chief's Remarks
content: The party, which won 108 seats in the polls and fell short of the majority mark of 118 seats, asserted that it did not need to "bargain or negotiate" with anyone for support.

title: AMMK Alleges 'Horse Trading' After MLA Goes Missing Amid Tamil Nadu Drama
content: Vijay's party TVK secured letters of support from the Congress, CPI and CPI(M), with the two Left parties extending outside support.

title: Google Settles Racial Discrimination Lawsuit For $50 Million
content: The suit claimed the company steered them into lower-level and lower-paid jobs and subjected them to a hostile work environment if they speak out.

title: 3-Year-Old Girl Raped By School Staff In Delhi; Accused Granted Bail
content: A three-year-old girl was allegedly raped by a 57-year-old staff member inside a private school in west Delhi's Janakpuri area, police said on Friday.

title: Did SIR Impact West Bengal result? What Election Commission Data Says
content: The numbers suggest that while there were indeed large-scale deletions of "ineligible" electors during the SIR process, there were also equally significant additions.

title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation LIVE: Vijay's TVK Loses Two Probable Allies, Its Hopes Now Rest On VCK
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: There is buzz that the Dravidian arch-rivals DMK and AIADMK may come together after over 50 years to form a government and keep Vijay out of power.

title: "No Repentance": Top Court On Minister's Colonel Sofiya Qureshi Remark
content: A bench of Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi observed that he should have apologised immediately over his remarks referring to Colonel Qureshi as a "sister of terrorists".

title: In Tamil Nadu Political Chaos, A 1997 Pramod Mahajan Speech Finds Relevance
content: The speech, delivered on April 11, 1997, during discussions on the survival of Prime Minister HD Deve Gowda's United Front government, is remembered not just for its sharp observation but also for its humour.

title: Marco Rubio Says US Ties With Church Can Withstand Trump-Pope Tensions
content: Marco Rubio, who visited the Vatican on Thursday, told reporters on Friday before leaving Rome the meeting was "very positive".

title: 'Could Be Forgery': AMMK Chief To NDTV On TVK Claiming Support Of His MLA
content: TTV Dhinakaran said he and his party's MLA, Kamraj, have signed the letter of support to the AIADMK and submitted it to the Governor.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: UAE Reports New Strikes As US-Iran Ceasefire Hangs By Thread
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: "Centre's Intervention May Be Needed": Top Court On Drugs Menace In Punjab
content: A bench comprising Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi referred to a recent incident involving a mother who lost all her five sons to drug addiction.

title: Amazon's Chile Data Centre To Be Built As Residents Lose Green Challenge
content: Amazon Web Services said it aims for the data centre to consume minimal energy and water and that its plan had met the environmental requirements.

title: "AIMIM Leader Had Role In Hiding Nida Khan": Devendra Fadnavis On TCS 'Conversion' Case
content: Nida Khan has been remanded to police custody till Monday, according to the complainant's lawyer, Advocate Milind Kurkute.

title: Opinion: Opinion | No Permanent Rivals In Politics As Vijay Turns Tamil Nadu Into 3-Way Battle
content: Back in 2006, when former Chief Minister J Jayalalithaa formed an alliance with MDMK chief Vaiko, she told waiting reporters that "there are no permanent friends or allies in politics."

title: Votes, Victory and Viral Interest: Vijay, TVK Ride High On Google Trends
content: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam has secured a majority in the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, a victory widely attributed to the star appeal of actor-turned-politician Vijay.

title: BJP Legislature Party To Pick New Assam Chief Minister On May 10
content: Assam caretaker Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma said the swearing-in ceremony of the new Assam government will be held on May 12 at 11 am.

title: 3 In Race, 1 Frontrunner: Inside Congress' Kerala Chief Minister Discussions
content: After the CLP meeting in Kerala, all newly elected Congress MLAs unanimously passed a one-line resolution authorising the party high command to choose the leader.

title: 'Trying To Harm Our Exports': Iran Seizes Oil Tanker In Gulf Of Oman
content: The oil tanker, which has been under US sanctions since February, was handed over to the judicial authorities.
